channel.title: Recent Articles in Phys. Rev. B
channel.description: Recent articles in Physical Review B
channel.dc:creator: rss@aps.org
channel.dc:rights: Copyright © 2026 the American Physical Society. Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited

item.title: Magnetoelastic honeycomb fragmentation in ${\mathrm{VI}}_{3}$
item.description:
Author(s): Enlin Shen, Tiberiu I. Popescu, Nishwal Gora, Guratinder Kaur, Edmond Chan, Harry Lane, Jose A. Rodriguez-Rivera, Guangyong Xu, Peter M. Gehring, Russell A. Ewings, Andy N. Fitch, and Chris Stock

Using a combination of neutron and x‑ray diffraction and neutron spectroscopy, the authors show here that VI3 undergoes a single structural transition at Ts ≈ 80 K followed by ferromagnetic order at Tc ≈ 50 K, producing two crystallographically inequivalent V3+ sites. They demonstrate that magnetoelastic coupling fragments the honeycomb lattice into two interpenetrating hexagonal sublattices, reshaping low‑temperature spin–orbital physics.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 014439] Published Thu Jan 29, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Enlin Shen, Tiberiu I. Popescu, Nishwal Gora, Guratinder Kaur, Edmond Chan, Harry Lane, Jose A. Rodriguez-Rivera, Guangyong Xu, Peter M. Gehring, Russell A. Ewings, Andy N. Fitch, and Chris Stock

Using a combination of neutron and x‑ray diffraction and neutron spectroscopy, the authors show here that VI3 undergoes a single structural transition at Ts ≈ 80 K followed by ferromagnetic order at Tc ≈ 50 K, producing two crystallographically inequivalent V3+ sites. They demonstrate that magnetoelastic coupling fragments the honeycomb lattice into two interpenetrating hexagonal sublattices, reshaping low‑temperature spin–orbital physics.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 014439] Published Thu Jan 29, 2026
item.dc:title: Magnetoelastic honeycomb fragmentation in ${\mathrm{VI}}_{3}$
item.dc:creator: Enlin Shen, Tiberiu I. Popescu, Nishwal Gora, Guratinder Kaur, Edmond Chan, Harry Lane, Jose A. Rodriguez-Rivera, Guangyong Xu, Peter M. Gehring, Russell A. Ewings, Andy N. Fitch, and Chris Stock

item.title: Berezinskii-Kosterlitz-Thouless phase transitions of the antiferromagnetic Ising model with ferromagnetic next-nearest-neighbor interactions on the kagome lattice
item.description:
Author(s): Yutaka Okabe and Hiromi Otsuka

We investigate the six-state clock universality of the Ising model on the kagome lattice, considering antiferromagnetic nearest-neighbor (NN) and ferromagnetic next-nearest-neighbor (NNN) interactions. Our comprehensive study employs three approaches: the level-spectroscopy method, Monte Carlo simul…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 014441] Published Thu Jan 29, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Yutaka Okabe and Hiromi Otsuka

We investigate the six-state clock universality of the Ising model on the kagome lattice, considering antiferromagnetic nearest-neighbor (NN) and ferromagnetic next-nearest-neighbor (NNN) interactions. Our comprehensive study employs three approaches: the level-spectroscopy method, Monte Carlo simul…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 014441] Published Thu Jan 29, 2026
item.dc:title: Berezinskii-Kosterlitz-Thouless phase transitions of the antiferromagnetic Ising model with ferromagnetic next-nearest-neighbor interactions on the kagome lattice

item.title: Benchmarking the effective many-body flat-band projected model of magic-angle bilayer graphene against the full tight-binding description
item.description:
Author(s): Miguel Sánchez Sánchez and Tobias Stauber

We benchmark the recently proposed projection method [M. Sánchez Sánchez et al., Phys. Rev. B 111, 205133 (2025)] for magic-angle twisted bilayer graphene (MATBG) across various symmetry-breaking phases at charge neutrality. The flat-band projected solutions agree well with the full tight binding, …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035155] Published Thu Jan 29, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Miguel Sánchez Sánchez and Tobias Stauber

We benchmark the recently proposed projection method [M. Sánchez Sánchez et al., Phys. Rev. B 111, 205133 (2025)] for magic-angle twisted bilayer graphene (MATBG) across various symmetry-breaking phases at charge neutrality. The flat-band projected solutions agree well with the full tight binding, …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035155] Published Thu Jan 29, 2026

item.title: Metal-insulator transition in a boundary three chain model
item.description:
Author(s): Niels John, Yuval Gefen, and Bernd Rosenow

We study the boundary physics of topologially trivial Mott insulators, representing the boundary by three coupled Hubbard chains in a linear confining potential. In the Hartree-Fock approximation, the ground state at and slightly off the particle-hole symmetric point remains insulating even at large…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035156] Published Thu Jan 29, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Niels John, Yuval Gefen, and Bernd Rosenow

We study the boundary physics of topologially trivial Mott insulators, representing the boundary by three coupled Hubbard chains in a linear confining potential. In the Hartree-Fock approximation, the ground state at and slightly off the particle-hole symmetric point remains insulating even at large…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035156] Published Thu Jan 29, 2026

item.title: Dissipation and interaction-controlled non-Hermitian skin effects
item.description:
Author(s): Yang Li, Zhao-Fan Cai, Tao Liu, and Franco Nori

Non-Hermitian skin effects (NHSEs) have recently been extensively studied at the single-particle level. When two-particle interactions become dominant, novel non-Hermitian phenomena can emerge. In this work, we propose an experimentally accessible mechanism to induce and control NHSEs in interacting…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035444] Published Thu Jan 29, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Yang Li, Zhao-Fan Cai, Tao Liu, and Franco Nori

Non-Hermitian skin effects (NHSEs) have recently been extensively studied at the single-particle level. When two-particle interactions become dominant, novel non-Hermitian phenomena can emerge. In this work, we propose an experimentally accessible mechanism to induce and control NHSEs in interacting…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035444] Published Thu Jan 29, 2026
item.dc:title: Dissipation and interaction-controlled non-Hermitian skin effects

item.dc:subject: Surface physics, nanoscale physics, low-dimensional systems
item.prism:section: Surface physics, nanoscale physics, low-dimensional systems

item.title: Nonreciprocal zone boundary magnon propagation in ${\mathrm{Cu}}_{2}{\mathrm{OSeO}}_{3}$
item.description:
Author(s): T. Weber, N. Heinsdorf, M. Stekiel, P. Steffens, A. P. Schnyder, and C. Pfleiderer

Here, the authors employ inelastic neutron scattering to show that the magnon spectrum of the chiral magnet Cu2OSeO3 features a strong directional dichroism at the boundary of the nuclear Brillouin zone. The affected magnon modes show virtually no damping and can be influenced using a magnetic field, making them of great interest for research on high-frequency unidirectional magnonic applications such as switchable spin-wave diodes.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L020414] Published Thu Jan 29, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): T. Weber, N. Heinsdorf, M. Stekiel, P. Steffens, A. P. Schnyder, and C. Pfleiderer

Here, the authors employ inelastic neutron scattering to show that the magnon spectrum of the chiral magnet Cu2OSeO3 features a strong directional dichroism at the boundary of the nuclear Brillouin zone. The affected magnon modes show virtually no damping and can be influenced using a magnetic field, making them of great interest for research on high-frequency unidirectional magnonic applications such as switchable spin-wave diodes.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L020414] Published Thu Jan 29, 2026
item.dc:title: Nonreciprocal zone boundary magnon propagation in ${\mathrm{Cu}}_{2}{\mathrm{OSeO}}_{3}$
item.dc:creator: T. Weber, N. Heinsdorf, M. Stekiel, P. Steffens, A. P. Schnyder, and C. Pfleiderer

item.title: Probing quantum geometric nonlinear magnetization via second-harmonic magneto-optical Kerr effect
item.description:
Author(s): Xuan Qian, Xiao-Bin Qiang, Wenkai Zhu, Yuqing Huang, Yiyuan Chen, Hai-Zhou Lu, Yang Ji, and Kaiyou Wang

Quantum geometry provides an intrinsic framework for characterizing the geometric structure of quantum states. It highlights its relevance to various aspects of fundamental physics. However, its direct implications for magnetic phenomena remain largely unexplored. Here, we report the observation of …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L041407] Published Thu Jan 29, 2026
item.content:encoded:

Quantum geometry provides an intrinsic framework for characterizing the geometric structure of quantum states. It highlights its relevance to various aspects of fundamental physics. However, its direct implications for magnetic phenomena remain largely unexplored. Here, we report the observation of …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L041407] Published Thu Jan 29, 2026

item.title: Fulde-Ferrell-Larkin-Ovchinnikov states and topological Bogoliubov Fermi surfaces in altermagnets: An analytical study
item.description:
Author(s): Zhao Liu, Hui Hu, and Xia-Ji Liu

We present an analytical study of the ground-state phase diagram for dilute two-dimensional spin-$\frac{1}{2}$ Fermi gases exhibiting $d$-wave altermagnetic spin splitting under $s$-wave pairing. Within the Bogoliubov–de Gennes mean-field framework, four distinct phases are identified: a Bardeen-Sch…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 024518] Published Wed Jan 28, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Zhao Liu, Hui Hu, and Xia-Ji Liu

We present an analytical study of the ground-state phase diagram for dilute two-dimensional spin-12 Fermi gases exhibiting d-wave altermagnetic spin splitting under s-wave pairing. Within the Bogoliubov–de Gennes mean-field framework, four distinct phases are identified: a Bardeen-Schrieffer-Cooper …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 024518] Published Wed Jan 28, 2026
item.dc:title: Fulde-Ferrell-Larkin-Ovchinnikov states and topological Bogoliubov Fermi surfaces in altermagnets: An analytical study

item.title: Superconducting diode effect in selectively grown topological insulator based Josephson junctions
item.description:
Author(s): Gerrit Behner, Abdur Rehman Jalil, Detlev Grützmacher, and Thomas Schäpers

The Josephson diode effect, where the switching current magnitude depends on its direction, arises when both time-reversal and inversion symmetries are broken, often achieved by a combination of spin-orbit interaction and applied magnetic fields. Taking advantage of the strong spin-orbit coupling in…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035440] Published Wed Jan 28, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Gerrit Behner, Abdur Rehman Jalil, Detlev Grützmacher, and Thomas Schäpers

The Josephson diode effect, where the switching current magnitude depends on its direction, arises when both time-reversal and inversion symmetries are broken, often achieved by a combination of spin-orbit interaction and applied magnetic fields. Taking advantage of the strong spin-orbit coupling in…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035440] Published Wed Jan 28, 2026

item.title: Current-induced molecular dissociation: Topological insulators as robust reaction platforms
item.description:
Author(s): Erika L. Mehring, Amparo Figueroa, Matias Berdakin, and Hernán L. Calvo

The growing interest in topological materials with symmetry-protected surface states as catalytic platforms has sparked the emerging field of topocatalysis. As robust transport is one of the key features of topological insulators, here we explore current-induced molecular dissociation in a transport…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035442] Published Wed Jan 28, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Erika L. Mehring, Amparo Figueroa, Matias Berdakin, and Hernán L. Calvo

The growing interest in topological materials with symmetry-protected surface states as catalytic platforms has sparked the emerging field of topocatalysis. As robust transport is one of the key features of topological insulators, here we explore current-induced molecular dissociation in a transport…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035442] Published Wed Jan 28, 2026

item.title: Magnetic injection photocurrents in valley-polarized states of twisted bilayer graphene
item.description:
Author(s): Fernando Peñaranda, Héctor Ochoa, and Fernando de Juan

Magic-angle twisted bilayer graphene displays a complex phase diagram as a function of flat-band filling, featuring compressibility cascade transitions and a variety of competing ground states with broken spin, valley, and point-group symmetries. Recent terahertz photocurrent spectroscopy experiment…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035443] Published Wed Jan 28, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Fernando Peñaranda, Héctor Ochoa, and Fernando de Juan

Magic-angle twisted bilayer graphene displays a complex phase diagram as a function of flat-band filling, featuring compressibility cascade transitions and a variety of competing ground states with broken spin, valley, and point-group symmetries. Recent terahertz photocurrent spectroscopy experiment…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035443] Published Wed Jan 28, 2026

item.title: Surface state dominated transport in HgTe topological insulator devices
item.description:
Author(s): M. Hofer, C. Fuchs, L. Fürst, T. Kießling, W. Beugeling, S. Schreyeck, H. Buhmann, and L. W. Molenkamp

We investigate $26–110\phantom{\rule{0.16em}{0ex}}\mathrm{nm}$-thick tensile strained HgTe films, which possess surface-state transport characteristic of three-dimensional topological insulators. The high transport mobility and low intrinsic doping of the material permits investigating the Landau le…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 045303] Published Wed Jan 28, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): M. Hofer, C. Fuchs, L. Fürst, T. Kießling, W. Beugeling, S. Schreyeck, H. Buhmann, and L. W. Molenkamp

We investigate 26–110nm-thick tensile strained HgTe films, which possess surface-state transport characteristic of three-dimensional topological insulators. The high transport mobility and low intrinsic doping of the material permits investigating the Landau level spectrum around the charge neutrali…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 045303] Published Wed Jan 28, 2026
item.dc:title: Surface state dominated transport in HgTe topological insulator devices

item.dc:subject: Semiconductors II: surfaces, interfaces, microstructures, and related topics
item.prism:section: Semiconductors II: surfaces, interfaces, microstructures, and related topics

item.title: Single-band triangular lattice Hubbard model with tunable anisotropy from twisted rhombic homobilayers
item.description:
Author(s): Wen Sun, Chuyi Tuo, and Hong Yao

The authors demonstrate here that twisted semiconductor homobilayers with band extrema at the Y valley on diamond lattices provide a promising platform for realizing a single-band triangular lattice Hubbard model with tunable hopping anisotropy via a displacement field. In the absence of a displacement field, the low-energy physics is governed by weakly coupled chains with quasi-one-dimensional band structure; increasing the displacement field generates interchain hopping, realizing a two-dimensional triangular lattice with tunable hopping anisotropy. To gain further insight into the role of hopping anisotropy, they employ density matrix renormalization group (DMRG) to explore the many-body physics of this model at U/t = 10 and half filling. The resulting phase diagram includes chiral spin liquid, nonmagnetic, and Néel antiferromagnetic phases, all accessible through displacement field control.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L041119] Published Wed Jan 28, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Wen Sun, Chuyi Tuo, and Hong Yao

The authors demonstrate here that twisted semiconductor homobilayers with band extrema at the Y valley on diamond lattices provide a promising platform for realizing a single-band triangular lattice Hubbard model with tunable hopping anisotropy via a displacement field. In the absence of a displacement field, the low-energy physics is governed by weakly coupled chains with quasi-one-dimensional band structure; increasing the displacement field generates interchain hopping, realizing a two-dimensional triangular lattice with tunable hopping anisotropy. To gain further insight into the role of hopping anisotropy, they employ density matrix renormalization group (DMRG) to explore the many-body physics of this model at U/t = 10 and half filling. The resulting phase diagram includes chiral spin liquid, nonmagnetic, and Néel antiferromagnetic phases, all accessible through displacement field control.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L041119] Published Wed Jan 28, 2026
item.dc:title: Single-band triangular lattice Hubbard model with tunable anisotropy from twisted rhombic homobilayers
item.dc:creator: Wen Sun, Chuyi Tuo, and Hong Yao
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Electronic structure and strongly correlated systems
item.prism:section: Electronic structure and strongly correlated systems

item.title: Thermodynamics and heat transport of a ${\mathrm{Yb}}_{3}{\mathrm{Sc}}_{2}{\mathrm{Ga}}_{3}{\mathrm{O}}_{12}$ single crystal: A quantum spin liquid candidate
item.description:
Author(s): Y. F. Xin, A. Rutherford, N. Li, M. L. Feng, Y. J. Liu, Z. Y. Zhao, Y. Y. Wang, H. Liang, Y. Zhou, Q. J. Li, M. Y. Xu, W. Xie, E. S. Choi, X. Zhao, J. Ma, H. D. Zhou, and X. F. Sun

Experimental realization of a quantum spin liquid is rare in three-dimensional frustrated magnets, in which quantum fluctuations are less prominent. Here, the authors investigate the ultralow-temperature magnetic susceptibility, specific heat, and thermal transport properties of a Yb3Sc2Ga3O12 single crystal, which is a hyperkagome lattice-based garnet. Their study demonstrates a disordered ground state with effective ½ spin and antiferromagnetic coupling in this three-dimensional frustrated material.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 014436] Published Tue Jan 27, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Y. F. Xin, A. Rutherford, N. Li, M. L. Feng, Y. J. Liu, Z. Y. Zhao, Y. Y. Wang, H. Liang, Y. Zhou, Q. J. Li, M. Y. Xu, W. Xie, E. S. Choi, X. Zhao, J. Ma, H. D. Zhou, and X. F. Sun

Experimental realization of a quantum spin liquid is rare in three-dimensional frustrated magnets, in which quantum fluctuations are less prominent. Here, the authors investigate the ultralow-temperature magnetic susceptibility, specific heat, and thermal transport properties of a Yb3Sc2Ga3O12 single crystal, which is a hyperkagome lattice-based garnet. Their study demonstrates a disordered ground state with effective ½ spin and antiferromagnetic coupling in this three-dimensional frustrated material.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 014436] Published Tue Jan 27, 2026
item.dc:title: Thermodynamics and heat transport of a ${\mathrm{Yb}}_{3}{\mathrm{Sc}}_{2}{\mathrm{Ga}}_{3}{\mathrm{O}}_{12}$ single crystal: A quantum spin liquid candidate

item.description:
Author(s): Leshan Zhao, Tong Chen, Matthew B. Stone, Qiang Zhang, Colin L. Sarkis, S. M. Koohpayeh, and Collin L. Broholm

We investigate the crystal electric field (CEF) excitations of ${\mathrm{Yb}}^{3+}$ ions in powder samples of the triangular-lattice rare-earth-based antiferromagnet ${\mathrm{YbZn}}_{2}{\mathrm{GaO}}_{5}$ using inelastic neutron scattering (INS). Three CEF excitations from the ground-state Kramers …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 014437] Published Tue Jan 27, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Leshan Zhao, Tong Chen, Matthew B. Stone, Qiang Zhang, Colin L. Sarkis, S. M. Koohpayeh, and Collin L. Broholm

We investigate the crystal electric field (CEF) excitations of Yb3+ ions in powder samples of the triangular-lattice rare-earth-based antiferromagnet YbZn2GaO5 using inelastic neutron scattering (INS). Three CEF excitations from the ground-state Kramers doublet were observed, each exhibiting signifi…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 014437] Published Tue Jan 27, 2026

item.title: Reentrant topology and reverse pumping in a quasiperiodic flux ladder
item.description:
Author(s): Sanchayan Banerjee, Rajashri Parida, and Tapan Mishra

Topological phases of matter are known to be unstable against strong onsite disorder in one dimension. In this work, however, we propose that in the case of a topological ladder, an onsite quasiperiodic potential under proper conditions, first destroys the initial topological phase (TP) and subseque…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 024205] Published Tue Jan 27, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Sanchayan Banerjee, Rajashri Parida, and Tapan Mishra

Topological phases of matter are known to be unstable against strong onsite disorder in one dimension. In this work, however, we propose that in the case of a topological ladder, an onsite quasiperiodic potential under proper conditions, first destroys the initial topological phase (TP) and subseque…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 024205] Published Tue Jan 27, 2026

item.title: Symmetry-breaking perturbations in the Jahn-Teller-Hubbard model
item.description:
Author(s): Natsuki Okada, Tatsuya Miki, Yusuke Nomura, Philipp Werner, and Shintaro Hoshino

We study the effect of symmetry-breaking perturbations in the multiorbital Hubbard model coupled to anisotropic Jahn-Teller phonons, which is relevant for the description of fulleride superconductors. This system is often approximated by a model with static antiferromagnetic (AFM) Hund's coupling, i…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 024517] Published Tue Jan 27, 2026
item.content:encoded:

We study the effect of symmetry-breaking perturbations in the multiorbital Hubbard model coupled to anisotropic Jahn-Teller phonons, which is relevant for the description of fulleride superconductors. This system is often approximated by a model with static antiferromagnetic (AFM) Hund's coupling, i…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 024517] Published Tue Jan 27, 2026

item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Superfluidity and superconductivity
item.prism:section: Superfluidity and superconductivity

item.title: Gapped boundaries of Kitaev's quantum double models: A lattice realization of anyon condensation from Lagrangian algebras
item.description:
Author(s): Mu Li, Xiao-Han Yang, and Xiao-Yu Dong

By establishing a direct correspondence between the axioms of Lagrangian algebras and consistency equations for boundary operators, the authors propose here a systematic framework for constructing all gapped boundaries of Kitaev’s quantum double models. This approach provides a microscopic characterization of bulk-to-boundary anyon condensation dynamics via the action of ribbon operators. Furthermore, the constructed lattice models naturally reproduce spontaneous symmetry-breaking patterns on the boundary, offering concrete realizations related to topological holography.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035150] Published Tue Jan 27, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Mu Li, Xiao-Han Yang, and Xiao-Yu Dong

By establishing a direct correspondence between the axioms of Lagrangian algebras and consistency equations for boundary operators, the authors propose here a systematic framework for constructing all gapped boundaries of Kitaev’s quantum double models. This approach provides a microscopic characterization of bulk-to-boundary anyon condensation dynamics via the action of ribbon operators. Furthermore, the constructed lattice models naturally reproduce spontaneous symmetry-breaking patterns on the boundary, offering concrete realizations related to topological holography.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035150] Published Tue Jan 27, 2026
item.dc:title: Gapped boundaries of Kitaev's quantum double models: A lattice realization of anyon condensation from Lagrangian algebras
item.dc:creator: Mu Li, Xiao-Han Yang, and Xiao-Yu Dong
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited

item.title: Electronic band structure of one-dimensional moiré chains
item.description:
Author(s): Dmitrii Vorobev, Yiheng Chen, and Grigory M. Tarnopolsky

We consider a general model of two atomic chains forming a moiré pattern due to a small mismatch in their lattice spacings, given by $θ=({a}_{1}−{a}_{2})/{a}_{2}$. Assuming arbitrary single-band dispersion relations ${ɛ}_{1}(p)$ and ${ɛ}_{2}(q)$ for the chains, along with an arbitrary interchain cou…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035154] Published Tue Jan 27, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Dmitrii Vorobev, Yiheng Chen, and Grigory M. Tarnopolsky

We consider a general model of two atomic chains forming a moiré pattern due to a small mismatch in their lattice spacings, given by θ=(a1−a2)/a2. Assuming arbitrary single-band dispersion relations ɛ1(p) and ɛ2(q) for the chains, along with an arbitrary interchain coupling term T(x), we show that t…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035154] Published Tue Jan 27, 2026
item.dc:title: Electronic band structure of one-dimensional moiré chains
item.dc:creator: Dmitrii Vorobev, Yiheng Chen, and Grigory M. Tarnopolsky
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Electronic structure and strongly correlated systems

item.title: Probing non-Hermitian effects on weak localization and antilocalization through dissipative topological surface states
item.description:
Author(s): Kai-He Ding, Lin Zhuang, and Wu-Ming Liu

The quantum interference effect arises from conduction electrons moving along time-reversed electron scattering paths in disordered systems, which leads to weak localization (WL) and antilocalization (WAL) behaviors that suppress and enhance the conductivity, respectively. The research on this effec…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035439] Published Tue Jan 27, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Kai-He Ding, Lin Zhuang, and Wu-Ming Liu

The quantum interference effect arises from conduction electrons moving along time-reversed electron scattering paths in disordered systems, which leads to weak localization (WL) and antilocalization (WAL) behaviors that suppress and enhance the conductivity, respectively. The research on this effec…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035439] Published Tue Jan 27, 2026
item.dc:title: Probing non-Hermitian effects on weak localization and antilocalization through dissipative topological surface states
item.dc:creator: Kai-He Ding, Lin Zhuang, and Wu-Ming Liu

item.dc:subject: Surface physics, nanoscale physics, low-dimensional systems
item.prism:section: Surface physics, nanoscale physics, low-dimensional systems

item.title: Raman scattering from moiré phonons
item.description:
Author(s): Vitor Dantas, Héctor Ochoa, Rafael M. Fernandes, and Natalia B. Perkins

We develop a theoretical framework for probing moiré phonon modes using Raman spectroscopy, and illustrate it with the example of twisted bilayer graphene (TBG). These moiré phonons arise from interlayer sliding motion in twisted two-dimensional materials and correspond to fluctuations of the stacki…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L041117] Published Tue Jan 27, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Vitor Dantas, Héctor Ochoa, Rafael M. Fernandes, and Natalia B. Perkins

We develop a theoretical framework for probing moiré phonon modes using Raman spectroscopy, and illustrate it with the example of twisted bilayer graphene (TBG). These moiré phonons arise from interlayer sliding motion in twisted two-dimensional materials and correspond to fluctuations of the stacki…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L041117] Published Tue Jan 27, 2026

item.title: Topological Dirac hourglass nodal line state in the superconductor TaOsSi
item.description:
Author(s): Lei Zhang, Wenyingdi He, Guangtao Wang, Dongyang Wang, and Zhenwei Wang

The topological quasiparticle states and superconductivity have attracted recent interest, especially when they coexist in a single material, offering a promising approach towards intrinsic topological superconductivity. Based on the first-principles calculations and symmetry analyses, we reveal tha…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L041118] Published Tue Jan 27, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Lei Zhang, Wenyingdi He, Guangtao Wang, Dongyang Wang, and Zhenwei Wang

The topological quasiparticle states and superconductivity have attracted recent interest, especially when they coexist in a single material, offering a promising approach towards intrinsic topological superconductivity. Based on the first-principles calculations and symmetry analyses, we reveal tha…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L041118] Published Tue Jan 27, 2026

item.title: Spin-Seebeck signatures of spin chirality in kagome antiferromagnets
item.description:
Author(s): Feodor Svetlanov Konomaev, Mithuss Tharmalingam, and Kjetil M. D. Hals

Noncollinear antiferromagnets (NCAFMs) are appealing for antiferromagnetic spintronics, as they combine the advantages of collinear antiferromagnets with novel emergent phenomena stemming from their complex spin structures. These phenomena are often associated with the intrinsic spin chirality, whic…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 014433] Published Mon Jan 26, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Feodor Svetlanov Konomaev, Mithuss Tharmalingam, and Kjetil M. D. Hals

Noncollinear antiferromagnets (NCAFMs) are appealing for antiferromagnetic spintronics, as they combine the advantages of collinear antiferromagnets with novel emergent phenomena stemming from their complex spin structures. These phenomena are often associated with the intrinsic spin chirality, whic…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 014433] Published Mon Jan 26, 2026

item.dc:subject: Magnetism

item.title: Manipulating non-Abelian anyons of Kitaev quantum spin liquids with local magnetic fields
item.description:
Author(s): Haoran Wang and Alessandro Principi

Non-Abelian anyonic excitations of quantum spin liquids have potential for application to topological quantum computation, but designing logical operations requires developing protocols to faithfully create, move, and read out such quasiparticles. In this paper, we test two such protocols for manipu…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 014434] Published Mon Jan 26, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Haoran Wang and Alessandro Principi

Non-Abelian anyonic excitations of quantum spin liquids have potential for application to topological quantum computation, but designing logical operations requires developing protocols to faithfully create, move, and read out such quasiparticles. In this paper, we test two such protocols for manipu…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 014434] Published Mon Jan 26, 2026

item.title: Topological pump and its plateau transitions of an $N$-leg spin ladder
item.description:
Author(s): Kota Yamamoto, Yoshihito Kuno, Tomonari Mizoguchi, Kazuki Sone, and Yasuhiro Hatsugai

A topological pump on an $N\text{−}\mathrm{leg}$ spin ladder is discussed by introducing spatial clusterization whose adiabatic limit is a set of $2N\text{−}\mathrm{site}$ staircase clusters. We define a pump path in parameter space that connects two distinct symmetry-protected topological phases. B…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 024432] Published Mon Jan 26, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Kota Yamamoto, Yoshihito Kuno, Tomonari Mizoguchi, Kazuki Sone, and Yasuhiro Hatsugai

A topological pump on an N−leg spin ladder is discussed by introducing spatial clusterization whose adiabatic limit is a set of 2N−site staircase clusters. We define a pump path in parameter space that connects two distinct symmetry-protected topological phases. By introducing a symmetry breaking st…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 024432] Published Mon Jan 26, 2026
item.dc:title: Topological pump and its plateau transitions of an $N$-leg spin ladder
item.dc:creator: Kota Yamamoto, Yoshihito Kuno, Tomonari Mizoguchi, Kazuki Sone, and Yasuhiro Hatsugai

item.title: Elementary building blocks for cluster Mott insulators
item.description:
Author(s): Vaishnavi Jayakumar and Ciarán Hickey

Mott insulators, in which strong Coulomb interactions fully localize electrons on single atomic sites, play host to an incredibly rich and exciting array of strongly correlated physics. One can naturally extend this concept to cluster Mott insulators, wherein electrons localize not on single atoms b…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035151] Published Mon Jan 26, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Vaishnavi Jayakumar and Ciarán Hickey

Mott insulators, in which strong Coulomb interactions fully localize electrons on single atomic sites, play host to an incredibly rich and exciting array of strongly correlated physics. One can naturally extend this concept to cluster Mott insulators, wherein electrons localize not on single atoms b…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035151] Published Mon Jan 26, 2026

item.title: Two-site entanglement in the two-dimensional Hubbard model
item.description:
Author(s): Frederic Bippus, Anna Kauch, Gergő Roósz, Christian Mayrhofer, Fakher Assaad, and Karsten Held

The study of entanglement in strongly correlated electron systems typically requires knowledge of the reduced density matrix. Here, we apply the parquet dynamical vertex approximation to study the two-site reduced density matrix at varying distance, in the Hubbard model at weak coupling. This allows…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035152] Published Mon Jan 26, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Frederic Bippus, Anna Kauch, Gergő Roósz, Christian Mayrhofer, Fakher Assaad, and Karsten Held

The study of entanglement in strongly correlated electron systems typically requires knowledge of the reduced density matrix. Here, we apply the parquet dynamical vertex approximation to study the two-site reduced density matrix at varying distance, in the Hubbard model at weak coupling. This allows…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035152] Published Mon Jan 26, 2026

item.title: Statistics of Abelian topological excitations
item.description:
Author(s): Hanyu Xue

In this paper, we develop a novel theory that generalizes the concept of anyon statistics to Abelian topological excitations of any dimension. We axiomatize excitations as a selected collection of states and operators satisfying the configuration axiom and the locality axiom, purely based on many-bo…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 045143] Published Mon Jan 26, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Hanyu Xue

In this paper, we develop a novel theory that generalizes the concept of anyon statistics to Abelian topological excitations of any dimension. We axiomatize excitations as a selected collection of states and operators satisfying the configuration axiom and the locality axiom, purely based on many-bo…

item.title: Spin susceptibility in a pseudogap state with fluctuating spiral magnetic order
item.description:
Author(s): Paulo Forni, Pietro M. Bonetti, Henrik Müller-Groeling, Demetrio Vilardi, and Walter Metzner

We compute the electron spin susceptibility in the pseudogap regime of the two-dimensional Hubbard model in the framework of a SU(2) gauge theory of fluctuating magnetic order. The electrons are fractionalized in fermionic chargons with a pseudospin degree of freedom and bosonic spinons. The chargon…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 045144] Published Mon Jan 26, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Paulo Forni, Pietro M. Bonetti, Henrik Müller-Groeling, Demetrio Vilardi, and Walter Metzner

We compute the electron spin susceptibility in the pseudogap regime of the two-dimensional Hubbard model in the framework of a SU(2) gauge theory of fluctuating magnetic order. The electrons are fractionalized in fermionic chargons with a pseudospin degree of freedom and bosonic spinons. The chargon…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 045144] Published Mon Jan 26, 2026

item.title: Quantum geometry from the Moyal product: Quantum kinetic equation and nonlinear response
item.description:
Author(s): Takamori Park, Xiaoyang Huang, Lucile Savary, and Leon Balents

We systematically derive the dissipationless quantum kinetic equation for a multiband free fermionic system with $U$(1) symmetry. Using the Moyal product formalism, we fully band diagonalize the dynamics. Expanding to the second order in gradients, which is beyond the semiclassical limit, we give a …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 045146] Published Mon Jan 26, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Takamori Park, Xiaoyang Huang, Lucile Savary, and Leon Balents

We systematically derive the dissipationless quantum kinetic equation for a multiband free fermionic system with U(1) symmetry. Using the Moyal product formalism, we fully band diagonalize the dynamics. Expanding to the second order in gradients, which is beyond the semiclassical limit, we give a co…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 045146] Published Mon Jan 26, 2026

item.title: Optical study on charge density waves in Cr-doped ${\mathrm{CsV}}_{3}{\mathrm{Sb}}_{5}$ kagome metals
item.description:
Author(s): Minjae Kim, Jaekyung Jang, Saqlain Yousuf, Hong Gu Lee, Yu-Seong Seo, Hanoh Lee, Tuson Park, and Jungseek Hwang

Many studies on ${\mathrm{CsV}}_{3}{\mathrm{Sb}}_{5}$ doped with various dopants have been conducted to explore the relationship between charge density waves (CDWs) and superconductivity. Among them, only Cr-doped ${\mathrm{CsV}}_{3}{\mathrm{Sb}}_{5}\phantom{\rule{4pt}{0ex}}[\mathrm{Cs}{({\mathrm{V}…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 045147] Published Mon Jan 26, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Minjae Kim, Jaekyung Jang, Saqlain Yousuf, Hong Gu Lee, Yu-Seong Seo, Hanoh Lee, Tuson Park, and Jungseek Hwang

Many studies on CsV3Sb5 doped with various dopants have been conducted to explore the relationship between charge density waves (CDWs) and superconductivity. Among them, only Cr-doped CsV3Sb5[Cs(V1−xCrx)3Sb5] exhibits a simultaneous weakening of the CDW and superconductivity as the doping concentrat…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 045147] Published Mon Jan 26, 2026
item.dc:title: Optical study on charge density waves in Cr-doped ${\mathrm{CsV}}_{3}{\mathrm{Sb}}_{5}$ kagome metals
item.dc:creator: Minjae Kim, Jaekyung Jang, Saqlain Yousuf, Hong Gu Lee, Yu-Seong Seo, Hanoh Lee, Tuson Park, and Jungseek Hwang
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Electronic structure and strongly correlated systems
item.prism:section: Electronic structure and strongly correlated systems

item.title: Electronic crystal phases in the presence of nonuniform Berry curvature and tunable Berry flux: The ${λ}_{N}$-jellium model
item.description:
Author(s): Félix Desrochers, Joe Huxford, Mark R. Hirsbrunner, and Yong Baek Kim

The authors introduce here a continuum model with a tunable Berry curvature profile and total Berry flux, to study electronic crystallization in geometrically nontrivial bands. Their numerical study of the model reveals sharp distinctions between topology driven by an underlying periodic potential and topology arising from spontaneous crystallization under strong electronic interactions. They shed light on this contrasting behavior using analytical arguments, including a general weak-potential formula for the Chern number and energetic considerations for anomalous Hall and halo Wigner crystals.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 045148] Published Mon Jan 26, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Félix Desrochers, Joe Huxford, Mark R. Hirsbrunner, and Yong Baek Kim

The authors introduce here a continuum model with a tunable Berry curvature profile and total Berry flux, to study electronic crystallization in geometrically nontrivial bands. Their numerical study of the model reveals sharp distinctions between topology driven by an underlying periodic potential and topology arising from spontaneous crystallization under strong electronic interactions. They shed light on this contrasting behavior using analytical arguments, including a general weak-potential formula for the Chern number and energetic considerations for anomalous Hall and halo Wigner crystals.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 045148] Published Mon Jan 26, 2026
item.dc:title: Electronic crystal phases in the presence of nonuniform Berry curvature and tunable Berry flux: The ${λ}_{N}$-jellium model
item.dc:creator: Félix Desrochers, Joe Huxford, Mark R. Hirsbrunner, and Yong Baek Kim
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited

item.title: Topological phase transitions and quarter-metal states of rhombohedral trilayer graphene
item.description:
Author(s): Hao-Sen Jin, Xi-Yue Wang, and Hang Xie

Rhombohedral trilayer graphene (RTG) hosts exotic electronic, magnetic, and superconductivity properties, offering a versatile platform to study correlated and topological phases. We systematically investigate topological phase transitions in RTG under optical fields and staggered electric potential…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 045149] Published Mon Jan 26, 2026

Rhombohedral trilayer graphene (RTG) hosts exotic electronic, magnetic, and superconductivity properties, offering a versatile platform to study correlated and topological phases. We systematically investigate topological phase transitions in RTG under optical fields and staggered electric potential…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 045149] Published Mon Jan 26, 2026

item.title: Chirality reversal at finite magnetic impurity strength and local signatures of a topological phase transition
item.description:
Author(s): Ruiqi Xu, Arnab Seth, and Itamar Kimchi

We study the honeycomb lattice with a single magnetic impurity modeled by adding imaginary next-nearest-neighbor hopping $ih$ on a single hexagon. This Haldane defect gives a topological mass term to the gapless Dirac cones and generates chirality. For a small density of defects, Neehus et al. [Phys…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 045151] Published Mon Jan 26, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Ruiqi Xu, Arnab Seth, and Itamar Kimchi

We study the honeycomb lattice with a single magnetic impurity modeled by adding imaginary next-nearest-neighbor hopping ih on a single hexagon. This Haldane defect gives a topological mass term to the gapless Dirac cones and generates chirality. For a small density of defects, Neehus et al. [Phys. …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 045151] Published Mon Jan 26, 2026

item.title: Charge current and phase diagram of the disordered open longer-range Kitaev chain
item.description:
Author(s): Emmanuele G. Cinnirella, Andrea Nava, and Domenico Giuliano

We compute the disorder averaged dc conductance in the non-equilibrium steady state that sets in between a longer-range Kitaev chain and a lead connected to an external reservoir, as a function of the system parameters and of the disorder strength. From our results, we map out the phase diagram of t…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 045152] Published Mon Jan 26, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Emmanuele G. Cinnirella, Andrea Nava, and Domenico Giuliano

We compute the disorder averaged dc conductance in the non-equilibrium steady state that sets in between a longer-range Kitaev chain and a lead connected to an external reservoir, as a function of the system parameters and of the disorder strength. From our results, we map out the phase diagram of t…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 045152] Published Mon Jan 26, 2026
item.dc:title: Charge current and phase diagram of the disordered open longer-range Kitaev chain
item.dc:creator: Emmanuele G. Cinnirella, Andrea Nava, and Domenico Giuliano
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Electronic structure and strongly correlated systems
item.prism:section: Electronic structure and strongly correlated systems

item.title: Quantum theory of optical spin texture in a chiral tellurium lattice
item.description:
Author(s): Pronoy Das, Sathwik Bharadwaj, Jungho Mun, Xueji Wang, Junsuk Rho, and Zubin Jacob

Chiral tellurium exhibits a giant optical gyrotropy whose microscopic origin has remained elusive. Here, the authors develop a quantum theory revealing that this phenomenon originates from spin-textured optical waves at the atomic scale. They develop the concept of deep microscopic optical band structure to demonstrate how the twisted lattice of tellurium lifts optical degeneracies for spinning optical waves. The theory captures quantitatively the superdispersive optical gyrotropy with excellent experimental agreement, unveiling hidden optical spin features analogous to electronic spin texture.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L041116] Published Mon Jan 26, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Pronoy Das, Sathwik Bharadwaj, Jungho Mun, Xueji Wang, Junsuk Rho, and Zubin Jacob

Chiral tellurium exhibits a giant optical gyrotropy whose microscopic origin has remained elusive. Here, the authors develop a quantum theory revealing that this phenomenon originates from spin-textured optical waves at the atomic scale. They develop the concept of deep microscopic optical band structure to demonstrate how the twisted lattice of tellurium lifts optical degeneracies for spinning optical waves. The theory captures quantitatively the superdispersive optical gyrotropy with excellent experimental agreement, unveiling hidden optical spin features analogous to electronic spin texture.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L041116] Published Mon Jan 26, 2026
item.dc:title: Quantum theory of optical spin texture in a chiral tellurium lattice
item.dc:creator: Pronoy Das, Sathwik Bharadwaj, Jungho Mun, Xueji Wang, Junsuk Rho, and Zubin Jacob
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited

item.title: Electronic structure reconstruction for $\sqrt{3}×\sqrt{3}$ charge density wave in heavily Cu-intercalated ${\mathrm{TiSe}}_{2}$ revealed by spatially resolved photoemission spectroscopy
item.description:
Author(s): G. Tomassucci, F. Minati, L. Tortora, M. Hattori, A. Barinov, M. Kopciuszynski, S. Kitou, H. Sawa, T. Mizokawa, and N. L. Saini

Scanning photoemission microscopy (SPEM) and angle-resolved photoemission spectroscopy (ARPES) are used to investigate the electronic structure of ${\mathrm{Cu}}_{0.33}{\mathrm{TiSe}}_{2}$ across the charge density wave (CDW) transition. The electronic structure is found to be largely homogeneous in…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035146] Published Fri Jan 23, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): G. Tomassucci, F. Minati, L. Tortora, M. Hattori, A. Barinov, M. Kopciuszynski, S. Kitou, H. Sawa, T. Mizokawa, and N. L. Saini

Scanning photoemission microscopy (SPEM) and angle-resolved photoemission spectroscopy (ARPES) are used to investigate the electronic structure of Cu0.33TiSe2 across the charge density wave (CDW) transition. The electronic structure is found to be largely homogeneous in the normal state above the CD…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035146] Published Fri Jan 23, 2026
item.dc:title: Electronic structure reconstruction for $\sqrt{3}×\sqrt{3}$ charge density wave in heavily Cu-intercalated ${\mathrm{TiSe}}_{2}$ revealed by spatially resolved photoemission spectroscopy

item.title: Uncovering surface states of the Dirac semimetal ${\mathrm{BaMg}}_{2}{\mathrm{Bi}}_{2}$
item.description:
Author(s): A. De Vita, J. Bakkelund, H. Świątek, M. J. Winiarski, S. Malick, C. V. B. Nielsen, F. Bertran, A. J. H. Jones, P. Majchrzak, F. Miletto Granozio, J. A. Miwa, R. Ernstorfer, T. Pincelli, T. Klimczuk, C. Bigi, and F. Mazzola

${\mathrm{BaMg}}_{2}{\mathrm{Bi}}_{2}$ is a Dirac semimetal characterized by a simple Dirac cone crossing the Fermi level at the center of the Brillouin zone, protected by ${C}_{3}$ rotational symmetry. Together with its Sr-based analog ${\mathrm{SrMg}}_{2}{\mathrm{Bi}}_{2}$, it has been proposed as…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035148] Published Fri Jan 23, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): A. De Vita, J. Bakkelund, H. Świątek, M. J. Winiarski, S. Malick, C. V. B. Nielsen, F. Bertran, A. J. H. Jones, P. Majchrzak, F. Miletto Granozio, J. A. Miwa, R. Ernstorfer, T. Pincelli, T. Klimczuk, C. Bigi, and F. Mazzola

BaMg2Bi2 is a Dirac semimetal characterized by a simple Dirac cone crossing the Fermi level at the center of the Brillouin zone, protected by C3 rotational symmetry. Together with its Sr-based analog SrMg2Bi2, it has been proposed as a promising candidate for a chemically driven topological switch: …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035148] Published Fri Jan 23, 2026
item.dc:title: Uncovering surface states of the Dirac semimetal ${\mathrm{BaMg}}_{2}{\mathrm{Bi}}_{2}$
item.dc:creator: A. De Vita, J. Bakkelund, H. Świątek, M. J. Winiarski, S. Malick, C. V. B. Nielsen, F. Bertran, A. J. H. Jones, P. Majchrzak, F. Miletto Granozio, J. A. Miwa, R. Ernstorfer, T. Pincelli, T. Klimczuk, C. Bigi, and F. Mazzola

item.title: Step defects in bismuth bromide ${\mathrm{Bi}}_{4}{\mathrm{Br}}_{4}$: Spatial reconfiguration and beam splitter of helical hinge states
item.description:
Author(s): Yan-Feng Zhou, Qi-Kai Ding, Yulei Han, Qing Yan, and Qing-Feng Sun

The quasi-one-dimensional material $α\text{−}{\mathrm{Bi}}_{4}{\mathrm{Br}}_{4}$ was recently predicted as a higher-order topological insulator with helical hinge states strongly dependent on the (001) surface termination. Here, we theoretically investigate how step defects on the (001) surface of $…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035435] Published Fri Jan 23, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Yan-Feng Zhou, Qi-Kai Ding, Yulei Han, Qing Yan, and Qing-Feng Sun

The quasi-one-dimensional material α−Bi4Br4 was recently predicted as a higher-order topological insulator with helical hinge states strongly dependent on the (001) surface termination. Here, we theoretically investigate how step defects on the (001) surface of α−Bi4Br4 nanowires reconfigure helical…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035435] Published Fri Jan 23, 2026
item.dc:title: Step defects in bismuth bromide ${\mathrm{Bi}}_{4}{\mathrm{Br}}_{4}$: Spatial reconfiguration and beam splitter of helical hinge states
item.dc:creator: Yan-Feng Zhou, Qi-Kai Ding, Yulei Han, Qing Yan, and Qing-Feng Sun
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Surface physics, nanoscale physics, low-dimensional systems
item.prism:section: Surface physics, nanoscale physics, low-dimensional systems

item.title: ${\mathbb{Z}}_{2}$ vortex crystals in tetrahedral antiferromagnets: Fractional charges and topological magnons
item.description:
Author(s): Tomoki Hirosawa, Alexander Mook, and Maria Azhar

We report the formation of a ${\mathbb{Z}}_{2}$ vortex crystal in the tetrahedral antiferromagnetic order on a triangular lattice. The noncoplanar tetrahedral state consists of four sublattices with spins oriented along the faces of a tetrahedron in spin space. The long-range order characterized by …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L020413] Published Fri Jan 23, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Tomoki Hirosawa, Alexander Mook, and Maria Azhar

We report the formation of a Z2 vortex crystal in the tetrahedral antiferromagnetic order on a triangular lattice. The noncoplanar tetrahedral state consists of four sublattices with spins oriented along the faces of a tetrahedron in spin space. The long-range order characterized by a Z2 topology ar…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L020413] Published Fri Jan 23, 2026
item.dc:title: ${\mathbb{Z}}_{2}$ vortex crystals in tetrahedral antiferromagnets: Fractional charges and topological magnons
item.dc:creator: Tomoki Hirosawa, Alexander Mook, and Maria Azhar
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Magnetism
item.prism:section: Magnetism

item.title: Integrable model of a superconductor with non-Fermi liquid and Mott phases
item.description:
Author(s): Santhosh M., Jorge Dukelsky, and Gerardo Ortiz

We present and analyze an exactly solvable interacting fermionic pairing model, which features interactions that entangle states at momenta $\mathbf{k}$ and $−\mathbf{k}$. These interactions give rise to correlated ground states, leading to a rich phase diagram that includes superconducting, multipl…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 014514] Published Thu Jan 22, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Santhosh M., Jorge Dukelsky, and Gerardo Ortiz

We present and analyze an exactly solvable interacting fermionic pairing model, which features interactions that entangle states at momenta k and −k. These interactions give rise to correlated ground states, leading to a rich phase diagram that includes superconducting, multiple metallic, and Mott-i…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 014514] Published Thu Jan 22, 2026
item.dc:title: Integrable model of a superconductor with non-Fermi liquid and Mott phases
item.dc:creator: Santhosh M., Jorge Dukelsky, and Gerardo Ortiz
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Superfluidity and superconductivity
item.prism:section: Superfluidity and superconductivity
